headline: Sabah State Election Called for August 2026
context: Sabah Governor dissolved the state assembly on March 15 triggering elections expected in August with GRS seeking to retain power.

cards.big: Sabah state assembly dissolved March 15 triggering August elections
cards.sub: 73 state seats contested with GRS defending its slim 38-seat majority

cards.big: GRS coalition won 38 of 73 seats in the 2020 snap election
cards.sub: Party-hopping and defections reduced working majority to just 3 seats

cards.big: Warisan under Shafie Apdal registered 28% support in February 2026 polls
cards.sub: Opposition targets rural Kadazan and Bajau seats lost in 2020

cards.big: Election Commission allocated RM180 million for Sabah state election
cards.sub: Remote polling stations in Tongod and Nabawan require helicopter ballot delivery

cards.big: State elections test whether Sabah voters can hold power to account
Sabah's August 2026 election will measure whether East Malaysian grievances translate into ballot outcomes.
